title: Malaxis linguella
intro: Malaxis linguella, also known as the tongue orchid, is a type of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and respiratory issues, due to its reported antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Malaxis linguella include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Malaxis linguella is often prepared as a tea or decoction, and it may also be found in capsules or tinctures, although its use and preparation can vary depending on the specific cultural or regional tradition.
